commit.message:
Implement jurisdiction-aware whitelist policy engine and compliance APIs (#515)

* Initial plan

* Add jurisdiction-aware WhitelistPolicy engine with fail-closed evaluation

- Add WhitelistPolicyModels.cs with InvestorCategory, WhitelistPolicyStatus,
  WhitelistPolicy, eligibility request/result, validation, and CRUD models
- Add IWhitelistPolicyService interface with 6 operations
- Add WhitelistPolicyService with in-memory storage and fail-closed semantics:
  Draft/Archived/empty policies always deny; denylist and blocked jurisdictions
  are hard-deny; all criteria satisfied yields Allow
- Add WhitelistPolicyController at /api/v1/whitelist/policies (7 endpoints:
  POST, GET all, GET by id, PUT, DELETE/archive, POST validate, POST evaluate)
- Extend WhitelistEntry and AddWhitelistEntryRequest with InvestorCategory and
  JurisdictionCode fields
- Register IWhitelistPolicyService singleton in Program.cs
- Add 41 tests covering CRUD, validation (contradiction detection), eligibility
  evaluation (fail-closed scenarios, happy path, jurisdiction/category checks),
  and HTTP API contract (auth guard, status codes, end-to-end lifecycle)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Implement jurisdiction-aware whitelist policy engine (Fixes #514)

Co-authored-by: ludovit-scholtz <256357527+ludovit-scholtz@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: ludovit-scholtz <256357527+ludovit-scholtz@users.noreply.github.com>

Adjust memory utilization thresholds to reduce false ALARM failures (#22764)

- Increase swss increase_threshold from 3% to 5% (heavy route operations
  like BGP update replication and link flap naturally cause 3-5% increase)
- Increase swss high_threshold from 8% to 10%
- Increase syncd increase_threshold from 5% to 7%
- Increase syncd high_threshold from 18% to 22% (TH2 platforms baseline
  is 9-10%, can reach 15-20% during heavy ASIC operations)
- Add Arista-7260CX3 hwsku group with higher syncd thresholds (8%/25%)
  since these TH2 broadcom platforms consistently show higher syncd memory

These thresholds caused 409 false failures across 49 test cases, 32 modules,
63 testbeds, and 17 HwSKUs in 30 days on 202511 branch.

Microsoft ADO: 37010532

Signed-off-by: Zhaohui Sun <zhaohuisun@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: sonic-mgmt

commit.message:
[qos] Fix broken S6100 T1 headroom pool skip condition (#22909)

PR #14912 incorrectly merged separate OR conditions into a single AND
condition, creating a logical contradiction:
  hwsku in ['Force10-S6100'] AND hwsku not in [..., 'Force10-S6100', ...]
This made the skip always FALSE, causing S6100 T1 headroom pool tests
to run and fail consistently since Oct 2024.

Restore the original separate OR conditions from PR #12266.

Fixes https://github.com/sonic-net/sonic-mgmt/issues/12292

Signed-off-by: Xu Chen <xuchen3@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: ludovit-scholtz <256357527+ludovit-scholtz@users.noreply.github.com>

Auto-retry interrupted agent turns after server restart

When a server restarts while the agent is mid-turn, the response is
left incomplete with no prompt_done. On session restore, detect this
condition (user_message with no subsequent prompt_done) and
automatically re-prompt the agent to continue.

The detection uses a new store.hasInterruptedTurn() query. The retry
fires from ws-handler after resumeSession() in the restore path,
setting busyKind so the client enters busy state immediately.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Sync missed events on iOS PWA foreground

When iOS backgrounds a PWA, the WebSocket can stay at readyState OPEN
while iOS suspends event delivery. Events broadcast during background
visible, if the WS is
still connected, call loadNewEvents() to catch up on missed events.

Also fix test infrastructure: capture real setTimeout in before() hook
instead of beforeEach() to prevent later tests from capturing the mock.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add 10 platform features — audit log, notifications, webhooks, approvals, export, branding, i18n, API playground, templates, real-time presence

Features implemented:

1. Audit Log (审计日志) — Event tracking with actor, action, resource, timestamps
2. Notification System (通知与@提及) — Bell icon, @mention detection, read/unread, preferences
3. Webhook & Integrations — CRUD webhooks, HMAC signing, delivery tracking, test endpoint
4. Approval Workflow (审批工作流) — Request/approve/reject flow, multi-reviewer, auto-resolution
5. Export Ecosystem (导出生态) — Markdown and HTML export with standalone theming
6. Custom Domain & Branding — Logo, colors, custom CSS, SEO metadata, favicon settings
7. Multi-language Docs (多语言文档管理) — Locale field, translation linking, locale switcher
8. Enhanced API Playground — Environments, auth configs, request history, CORS proxy
9. Template Library (模板市场) — 6 built-in templates, create-from-template, category browsing
10. Real-time Presence (实时协同) — Active editor tracking, presence avatars with heartbeat

Infrastructure:
- 14 new database tables with full Drizzle ORM schema and relations
- 8 new enums for status types
- 9 new query modules with business logic
- 27 new API route files
- 7 new UI components + 1 custom hook
- 5 new pages (audit logs, webhooks, approvals, branding, templates)
- Navigation integration in top nav with notification bell
- Editor integration with export menu, locale switcher, and presence avatars
- Template picker in document list page

fix: redesign comment flow for seamless select-comment-submit UX

Before: clicking Comment in bubble menu showed a toast telling users
to manually open the sidebar — a dead-end UX.

After: Select text → click Comment → sidebar auto-opens with a
pre-filled form showing the quoted text → type comment → submit →
CommentMark highlight is applied to the text range in the editor.

Changes:
- Add CommentSelection type + onComment callback to DocEditorProps
- Add applyCommentMark method to DocEditorHandle
- Editor captures selection (from/to/text) on comment action
- Editor page auto-opens sidebar + passes pendingComment context
- Sidebar shows NewCommentForm at top when pending comment exists
- After submit, CommentMark is applied to the text range
- Keyboard shortcuts: ⌘+Enter to submit, Escape to cancel
- Reply form also supports ⌘+Enter and Escape
- Resolve badge uses tone tokens instead of hardcoded emerald

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(matter): resolve SBMD endpoints by device type matching (#174)

The original method used to locate the Matter device endpoint to use for
a resource originated from the method used by Zigbee. Zigbee doesn't
have the device types and parts list attributes which are much better
suited to locate endpoints hosting desired functionality. Our drivers
are for specific device types (on/off light, dimmable light, etc). This
maps directly to the device types attribute on Matter endpoints and is
more reliable than just looking for an endpoint with a target cluster as
before.

- Add endpoint map (sbmdEndpointMap) to MatterDevice, built during
AddDevice() by matching device type lists against driver's
matterMeta.deviceTypes
- Add ResolveEndpointMap() and GetEndpointForSbmdIndex() methods
- Update BindResourceReadInfo, BindWriteInfo, BindExecuteInfo, and
BindResourceEventInfo to accept optional sbmdEndpointIndex parameter
- Pre-resolve Matter endpoint IDs at bind time; device-level resources
fall back to GetEndpointForCluster()
- Store resolvedEndpointId on write/execute bindings for use at
invocation time
- Update SpecBasedMatterDeviceDriver to pass endpoint index for
endpoint-level resources and std::nullopt for device-level resources
- Add MatterDeviceEndpointMapTest with 27 tests covering endpoint map
resolution, index lookup, and all four binding methods
- Add device-type-endpoint-resolution spec to openspec/specs
- Archive endpoints-from-device-types change

Refs: BARTON-358

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix empty session cleanup: use time-based threshold instead of liveSessions exclusion

deleteEmptySessions previously excluded all liveSessions from cleanup,
but liveSessions only grows (never shrinks except on explicit delete),
so empty sessions were never actually cleaned up.

Replace the exclusion-based approach with a time-based one: only delete
empty sessions older than 60 seconds. This avoids the race condition
where another client might still be viewing a freshly created empty
session, while ensuring abandoned empty sessions are eventually cleaned.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- F3: Global full-text search (PostgreSQL tsvector, Cmd+K search dialog)
- F6: Automated changelog generation from OpenAPI diffs

Phase 2 — Enhancement:
- F2: Multi-version management (api_versions table, version switcher)
- F4: Polyglot code generation (httpsnippet: cURL/Python/JS/Node/Go)

Phase 3 — Collaboration:

fix(Windows): makechild.mk が子ディレクトリに適用されない問題を修正

[問題1] cygpath -w によるパス形式の不整合 (prepare.mk)
MAKEPART_MK の取得で cygpath -w を使用するとバックスラッシュ形式になり、
patsubst %/makepart.mk,%/makechild.mk のマッチが失敗して makechild.mk が
一切インクルードされなかった。
cygpath -m (D:/... 形式のフォワードスラッシュ) に変更して修正。

この修正により libsrc サブフォルダの makechild.mk に設定された
NO_LINK = 1 が子ディレクトリに正しく伝播されるようになった。

[問題2] NO_LINK=1 のとき clean がライブラリを削除対象に含める問題 (makelibsrc_c_cpp.mk)
NO_LINK=1 のディレクトリはライブラリを生成しないにも関わらず、
clean ターゲットが libapi.dll 等を rm 対象として表示していた (Linux/Windows 共通)。
CLEAN_COMMON/CLEAN_OS の構築を NO_LINK の有無で分岐して修正。

[問題3] DLL リンク時に OBJDIR が未作成で .exp mv が失敗する問題 (makelibsrc_c_cpp.mk)
ソースファイルを持たないライブラリディレクトリでは obj/md が作られず、
.exp ファイルの移動が失敗した。リンクルールの order-only 前提条件に
\ を追加して修正。

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas (#2)

* fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas

SummarizeRequest and SummarizeAgainRequest had `percentage: int` with
`ge=30, le=80`, which rejected `0` or `null` from the frontend with a
422 Unprocessable Entity.

Changed both to `int | None` with `ge=1, le=100` so callers can omit
or null-out the field; `_get_percentage()` already handles the
default fallback to a random 30-80 value.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: widen percentage range to 1-100 across models and simplify fallback

- Text model: percentage ge=30,le=80 → ge=1,le=100
- Summary model: same range update
- _get_percentage: replace random 30-80 fallback with deterministic
  default of 50%; respect user's explicit choice via schema validation
- Remove unused `random` import

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: add GitHub Actions workflow to auto-deploy to FastAPI Cloud

Triggers on every push to main. Installs fastapi-cli, restores the
auth token from the FASTAPI_CLOUD_AUTH GitHub secret, then runs
`fastapi deploy . --no-wait` which uses the app_id from .fastapicloud/cloud.json.

Setup required:
1. Copy the contents of ~/.fastapi/auth.json on your local machine
2. Add it as a GitHub secret named FASTAPI_CLOUD_AUTH in the repo settings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: fix deploy workflow to use uv + PAT token auth

- Use uv sync to install deps (matches project toolchain)
- Authenticate with `fastapi cloud login --token` using PAT
- Remove incorrect auth.json approach
- Run fastapi via `uv run` to use project venv

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: recreate .fastapicloud/cloud.json in CI before deploy

Since .fastapicloud is gitignored, the folder won't exist after
checkout. Recreate cloud.json from GitHub secrets so `fastapi deploy .`
can find the app_id and team_id.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Document cluster node health feature (#6502)

Adds documentation for the node health status feature implemented in PRs #4758 and #4767,
which enables Valkey-compatible cluster node state tracking.

Important: Node health is passive metadata provided by the cluster manager (control plane) via DFLYCLUSTER CONFIG.
Dragonfly nodes do not actively determine their own health status.

- Enable table resizing (resizable: true)
- Add image paste (clipboard) and drop handler with base64 encoding
- Fix Cmd+K link shortcut: use editor commands instead of querySelector DOM hack
- Add Cmd+Shift+X strikethrough shortcut

Round 2 — Mobile & Accessibility:
- Add role=toolbar with aria-label/orientation to formatting toolbar
- Add ARIA live region for screen reader announcements
- Announce editor mode switches and AI result completion
- Fix bubble menu link action to use editor command (no more DOM hack)

Round 3 — Code Quality:
- Extract 3 custom nodes (JsonViewer, SchemaViewer, Heatmap) to lib/editor/custom-nodes.ts
- Extract image paste/drop extension to lib/editor/image-paste-drop.ts
- doc-editor.tsx: 1597 → 1483 lines

Update pricewatch/db/config.py

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: consolidate constants, remove legacy code, fix anti-patterns

- Extract shared constants (VALID_STATUSES, MAX_*) to lib/constants.ts
  eliminating duplication across 6+ API routes
- Unify wordCount to lib/utils.ts (was duplicated in 3 files)
- Remove docs-url.ts (trivial encodeURIComponent wrapper), inline to documents.ts
- Remove getDocEditorShellWidth (no-op wrapper around clampDocEditorWidth)
- Remove OPENAI_* env var fallbacks in ai-server.ts, keep only AIHUBMIX_*
- Unify AI action definitions: single AI_ACTIONS map with mode metadata
  replaces separate AI_TRANSFORM/REWRITE/APPEND arrays
- Delete localStorage legacy doc migration code (~100 lines)
- Simplify document-summary-queue internal naming
- Replace search trigger DOM hack (hidden button + querySelector)
  with React Context (SearchOpenContext)
- Fix ThemeToggle to use shared useMounted hook
- Fix useToast listener leak: remove [state] dependency → []
- Fix useIsMobile SSR: boolean initial state instead of undefined
- Add pagination (limit/offset) to listDocuments query
- Add deterministic ordering to getUserWorkspaces (orderBy createdAt)
- Upgrade tsconfig target ES6 → ES2022
- Clean up .env.example (remove OPENAI_* fallback vars)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use ISOWeek.GetYear() for weekly push filename to handle year boundaries

Using range.From.Year produces wrong filenames for late-December dates
that belong to the next year's ISO week 1 (e.g., Dec 30 2024 is 2025-W01).

- Fix ReportsHandler.PushWeeklySummaryAsync to use ISOWeek.GetYear()
- Add regression test PushWeeklySummaryAsync_YearBoundary_UsesIsoYear

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
runtime-update: org.altlinux.Tuner 48 → 49 (#12)

* Initial plan

* fix(tuner): set x-version to 0.5.0

Flathub confirms current release is 0.5.0 on runtime 48. Our manifest
targets runtime 49 with tag 0.5.0 / commit 81f377d. Setting x-version
triggers CI on this PR branch.

Assisted-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 via OpenCode

* fix(tuner): add -Dgjs=false to libpeas, add missing lint exceptions

libpeas 2.0.7 requires mozjs-128 for its GJS loader, which is not
present in org.gnome.Platform/49. Disable with -Dgjs=false.

Also adds the standard non-Flathub lint exceptions that fire on
builddir/repo lint stages (appstream-no-flathub-manifest-key,
appstream-screenshots-not-mirrored-in-ostree,
appstream-external-screenshot-url).

Assisted-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 via OpenCode

---------

Implements all 18 interactive features:
- Preloader (1.5s) + boot sequence with 16 terminal lines
- Custom cursor with lerp outer tracking and hover states
- Matrix rain canvas at 30fps with katakana/number/symbol chars
- Particles system (80/40 on mobile) with mouse repulsion and connection lines

- Navbar scroll class, active section tracking, hamburger menu
- IntersectionObserver scroll animations with stagger delays
- Animated counters (easeOutCubic, 2000ms)
- Skill/platform bar animations (1500ms easing)

- Full terminal easter egg with 17 commands (neofetch, nmap, ping, history, etc.)
- Konami code (↑↑↓↓←→←→BA) to open terminal
- Contact form validation with real-time error clearing and toast feedback
- Toast notifications with slide-in animation and auto-dismiss

Complete real backend auth and deployment lifecycle contracts for MVP sign-off (#503)

* Initial plan

* Add AuthenticationService and BackendDeploymentLifecycle unit tests, fix JWT claim type bug

- Add BackendDeploymentLifecycleContractServiceUnitTests.cs (109 tests) covering:
  ARC76 deployment initiation, idempotency, validation, state transitions,
  audit trails, address derivation, and supported standards/networks
- Add AuthenticationServiceUnitTests.cs (37 tests) covering:
  registration, login, token refresh, logout, JWT validation, password change,
  derivation verification, session inspection, and ARC76 determinism
- Fix ValidateAccessTokenAsync bug: JwtSecurityToken.Claims returns short JWT
  claim names (e.g. 'nameid') not full URI claim types (ClaimTypes.NameIdentifier),
  so also check for 'nameid' short name in token validation

commit.message:
Add complete cyberpunk/terminal style.css for EpicNoob22 portfolio

- CSS custom properties for neon/dark theme
- Reset, base styles, custom scrollbar, ::selection
- Custom cursor (outer ring + inner dot, hover state)
- Preloader with spinner and boot sequence lines
- CRT scanlines overlay and matrix/particles canvas
- Glassmorphism navbar with scroll state and mobile hamburger
- Animate-on-scroll with stagger delays
- Hero section: glitch title with ::before/::after clip-path animations,
  terminal box, status badge, floating badges, avatar ring, scroll indicator
- About: two-column grid, timeline, stats grid, interest tags
- Skills: 3-column cards with progress bars and shimmer effect
- Projects: auto-fill grid with 3D tilt, project links
- Certifications: 2-column cards with neon-yellow accent
- CTF section: platforms grid, writeup cards, difficulty badges
- Blog: 2-column cards with purple accent
- Contact: social links, form with validation states, submit button
- Footer with back-to-top button
- Terminal easter egg overlay and toggle button
- Toast notifications with slide-in animation
- All keyframes: glitchTop, glitchBottom, blink, bounce, pulse,
  spin, float, scanlineMove, fadeInLine, matrixFall, shimmer,
  slideInToast, countUp
- Responsive breakpoints at 1200px, 768px, and 480px
